title: Living in Boulder vlog 9 - 22 - 2018: US Law Code

enrichment.summary: Kyle gives a late-night/early-morning update after a week away from videos. He describes his ongoing project of formatting the US Law Code (and other legal documents like the Colorado Constitution and Boulder City Charter) into a readable, text-to-speech-friendly book format on his website. He also recounts a neighborhood incident involving a screaming woman and a suspicious car, shares a phone repair saga, and briefly muses on capitalism, greed, and his theory that rewarding non-monetary effort could fix societal problems.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.topic: US Law Code as readable book
stances.position: Worth doing — stripping extraneous content and making it text-to-speech friendly is the best way to learn it
The fix is rewarding people for hard work and knowledge rather than money-making, which redirects energy toward improving the world
Addicted but not concerned — notes he has lost weight
Uses it but acknowledges it can cause mistakes when working; prefers medical-grade

entities: Decline and Fall of the Roman Empire

claims.claim: I live in Boulder, Vila 7 and 9 in the morning.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I haven't gone to sleep yet.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I just had breakfast, a wrap from Whole Foods.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I love roasted seaweed, especially the sesame kind.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I got two packages of roasted seaweed, but the one made for wraps isn't as good because it lacks salt.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I haven't done a video in a week.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I was working on history books, including 'Decline and Fall of the Roman Empire'.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I have six volumes of 'Decline and Fall of the Roman Empire'.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I decided to switch to working on the US Constitution because I have so many important books to cover.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I'm formatting the US Constitution from the Cornell website in my own way because their version is confusing.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: The US Constitution project is huge, with 54 chapters, and it might take a month or more.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I want to also do the Colorado Constitution and the Boulder City Charter.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I see value in having many books formatted the same way for easier learning.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I format books so they can be read aloud by a computer, which I think is an interesting concept.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I'm motivated by seeing how the final product will look, not just by money or making it for others.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: The US Constitution has many subsections, which I organize with numbers and color-coding.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I worry about my computer overheating due to the complexity of the tables I'm creating.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I was listening to 'Origins of Totalitarianism' while working.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I had issues with my iPhone 10 after a software reboot and screen replacement.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I heard a woman screaming outside and thought she might have had her keys or phone stolen.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I've been drinking energy drinks and lost weight, now weighing 112 pounds.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I thought the US Constitution project would take a week, but it might take two weeks or more.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I made a mistake in pasting code and had to delete hours of work.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I use a smoke stick, which I think is better for my lungs than flour.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I'm updating my video to talk about working on the US Constitution.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I was listening to Richard Wolff and Robert Tainter, whose work I find interesting.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I believe the solution to greed is to reward people for knowledge and hard work, not just money.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I think the world can be fixed by redirecting energy from greed to improving the world.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I'm yawning a lot and think it's time to sleep.
